title: Pointer Pointer
description: A strangely specific site that finds a photo of someone pointing exactly at your cursor.

title: Cat Bounce
description: Cats bounce around the screen. That is basically the whole point, and somehow it is enough.

title: Staggering Beauty
description: Wiggle your mouse and a tiny black creature dances wildly on the screen.
last_error: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate (_ssl.c:1010)

title: Find the Invisible Cow
description: Find an invisible cow by listening to the sound. Pointless, simple, oddly addictive.

title: Long Doge Challenge
description: Stretch Doge forever and test how much scrolling your patience can survive.

title: The Zen Zone
description: A set of tiny interactive spaces for zoning out, breathing, and doing almost nothing.

title: Checkbox Race
description: Race other people by clicking checkboxes. Not important. Very clicky.

title: Scream Into The Void
description: Type what you want to scream and throw it into the void. No reply. Very freeing.

title: Neal.fun
description: A collection of seriously well-made little web toys that can steal half an hour instantly.

title: Quick, Draw!
description: Draw badly and let Google’s AI guess what your sketch is supposed to be.

title: Window Swap
description: Look through random windows around the world and pretend you are somewhere else.

title: Radio Garden
description: Spin the globe and listen to live radio stations from around the world.

title: Always Judge a Book by Its Cover
description: A gallery proving that some books absolutely can be judged by their covers.

title: Mondrian and Me
description: Drag, click and play with Mondrian-style blocks for no practical reason.

title: Zoomquilt
description: An infinite zooming visual tunnel that may make you forget what you were doing.

title: Koalas to the Max
description: Split circles again and again until a koala appears. The process is the point.

title: rrrgggbbb
description: A mysterious red-green-blue playground. No instructions, just click around.

title: Hacker Typer
description: Mash your keyboard and instantly look like a Hollywood hacker.

title: Bees Bees Bees
description: A gloriously silly burst of bees, dancing, and internet nonsense. Exactly as useful as it sounds.

title: Paper Toilet
description: Drag the toilet paper roll forever. A tiny masterpiece of pointless interaction.

title: Binary Piano
description: Turn binary switches into little piano sounds and pretend you discovered a very tiny instrument.

title: Falling Falling
description: A hypnotic falling color-and-sound loop that feels like the internet slowly melting.

title: Ducks Are The Best
description: Move your cursor and unleash an unreasonable number of ducks. Hard to explain, easy to enjoy.

title: Heeeeeeeey
description: One of the shortest, dumbest, most charming websites online. It says what it needs to say.

title: Random Colour
description: A full-screen random color. Refresh your mood one color at a time.

title: Drawing Garden
description: Draw gentle animated garden shapes in the browser and zone out for a while.
Slide numbered tiles until you lose track of time. A classic quick browser game.

title: Flexbox Froggy
description: A cute CSS game where frogs teach layout. Slightly useful, but still fun enough to belong here.

title: Server Survival
description: A tower-defense style web game about surviving traffic with infrastructure. Nerdy and playable.

title: Cut Bread
description: Cut bread slices and see how evenly you did. A strangely specific skill nobody asked for.

title: WebGL Fluid Simulation
description: Swirl colorful digital fluid around your screen. Beautiful, relaxing, and completely nonessential.

title: CSS Doodle
description: A playground for generative CSS patterns and visual experiments. Great for creative wandering.

title: Tower Game
description: Stack a tiny tower block by block and watch your confidence collapse one pixel at a time.

title: URL Snake
description: Play Snake in a way that makes your browser address bar feel weirdly involved.

title: Polygon Shredder
description: Slice a 3D shape into digital confetti and enjoy the very specific joy of controlled destruction.

title: Patatap
description: Press keys to trigger bright animations and punchy sounds. Instant keyboard-powered nonsense.

title: Sandspiel
description: Paint with sand, water, fire, plants and other tiny simulated chaos in your browser.

title: Bongo Cat
description: Tap keys and turn a simple cat into a tiny browser band for a very short music break.

title: OMFGDOGS
description: A rainbow stampede of looping pixel dogs that is loud, silly, and proudly unnecessary.

title: MapCrunch
description: Jump to random Google Street View places and pretend your next tiny adventure starts there.

title: Windows 93
description: A fake retro operating system packed with strange apps, jokes, sounds, and clickable nonsense.

title: The Nicest Place on the Internet
description: A soft corner of the web that offers quiet video hugs when you need a gentle reset.

title: Thisissand
description: A calm sand-painting toy where colored grains pile up into oddly satisfying landscapes.

title: A Soft Murmur
description: Mix rain, waves, birds, thunder, and other ambient sounds into your own tiny background world.

title: Paper Planes
description: Fold and stamp a virtual paper plane, then throw it into a shared globe of drifting planes.

title: Emoji Kitchen
description: Combine emoji into ridiculous sticker mashups and browse the results for no practical reason.

title: ZType
description: A typing shooter where words become targets. It feels productive for about ten seconds, then turns into pure arcade focus.

title: Little Alchemy 2
description: Combine tiny ideas into hundreds of strange discoveries. A clean browser toy for people who like clicking just one more time.

title: This Person Does Not Exist
description: Refresh to see a face generated by AI. Weird, quick, and slightly uncanny.

title: Internet Live Stats
description: Watch internet counters tick upward in real time. Not exactly useful for your day, but strangely hard to stop watching.

title: 100,000 Stars
description: A beautiful interactive map of nearby stars that turns a short break into a tiny space trip.

title: Quick, Draw! Dataset Viewer
description: Browse thousands of rough doodles people made while Google tried to guess them.

title: Experiments with Google
description: A huge gallery of playful web, AI, Chrome and art experiments for people who want the internet to feel surprising again.

title: Make Everything OK
description: Press a button that promises to make everything OK. The joke is simple, and that is the point.

title: Infinite Craft
description: Combine words into stranger and stranger things. A deceptively endless browser rabbit hole.

title: Absurd Trolley Problems
description: A sequence of increasingly ridiculous moral choices from Neal.fun.

title: The Bored Button
description: Press a button and get sent to a quick random activity or internet distraction for bored moments.

title: Chrome Music Lab
description: Tiny music experiments that turn rhythm, melody and sound into playful browser toys.

title: Sandboxels
description: A falling-sand sandbox with hundreds of materials, reactions, fire, water, lava and delightful pixel chaos.

title: Close Hue
description: A daily color guessing game where you match names to hues and learn how off your instincts are.

title: Kinda Hard Golf
description: A tiny daily golf challenge about launching a ball up awkward hills with just enough frustration to be funny.

title: Just Hit The Button
description: A supposedly simple button pressing game that keeps turning one obvious task into a silly retry loop.

title: That’s The Finger
description: A single rude pixel hand follows your cursor. It is childish, pointless, and very much the internet.

title: Paint Toys
description: A playful set of browser painting tools for making quick colorful messes.

title: AutoDraw
description: Doodle badly and let machine learning turn the sketch into cleaner icons and drawings.

title: Pug in a Rug
description: A pug, a rug, and a short silly moment that asks absolutely nothing from you.

title: The Useless Web
description: The classic random button for useless websites, included as a reference point for the whole genre.

title: Cache Monet
description: Hypnotic screen-saver patterns for people who miss the old weird web.

title: Is It Christmas?
description: A one-question website with the kind of confidence most apps can only dream of.

title: Rainy Mood
description: Instant rain ambience for pretending your short break is cinematic and peaceful.

title: Sanger.dk
description: A dog licks your screen. That is the whole website, and somehow it still deserves a place here.

title: Nyan Cat
description: The rainbow cat loop that helped define a very specific era of pointless internet joy.

title: Neonflames
description: Paint glowing nebula-like light trails in the browser and watch the screen turn into calm digital smoke.

title: Draw a Stickman
description: Draw a little stick figure and send it through a playful interactive story built around your sketch.

title: Corn Dog on Corn Dog
description: A looping wall of dancing corn dogs. It explains itself instantly and then keeps going.

title: Passive Aggressive Passwords
description: Type a password and receive unnecessarily judgmental feedback from a tiny passive-aggressive web toy.

title: Smash The Walls
description: Click to smash digital brick walls when your brain wants a harmless tiny burst of destruction.

title: Orb.Farm
description: Build a tiny virtual aquarium ecosystem with algae, fish, sand, glass, water and gentle pixel-life chaos.

title: JS Paint
description: A nostalgic browser paint app for making quick pixel messes, doodles and retro interface art without installing anything.

title: Cross Divisions
description: Move through a hypnotic field of shifting lines and color until the screen feels like a tiny optical detour.

title: Kandinsky by Chrome Music Lab
description: Draw shapes and hear them turn into sound in a colorful browser experiment inspired by Kandinsky.

title: Bouncy Balls
description: Watch colorful balls bounce in response to noise or movement, a classroom tool that also works as a quick visual toy.

title: Vectorpark
description: A gallery of strange interactive browser sketches, tiny games, and surreal web toys by Patrick Smith.

title: Checkboxes
description: A grid of checkboxes that turns ordinary form controls into a surprisingly satisfying browser toy.

title: Maze Toys
description: A tiny collection of browser mazes and toy-like puzzles for a quick low-stakes distraction.

title: ToneMatrix
description: Click squares on a minimalist grid to create a looping melody in seconds.

title: Blob Opera
description: A charming Google Arts & Culture experiment where singing blobs let anyone compose a tiny opera in the browser.

title: Hacker Typer
description: Mash your keyboard and instantly look like a movie hacker writing mysterious code.

title: Clicking Toys
description: A polished pile of tiny click-first browser toys, buttons, widgets, and playful micro-interactions.

title: Ball Pool by Mr.doob
description: Drag and fling colorful browser balls around a simple physics sandbox for a quick satisfying break.

title: Lines Chrome Experiment
description: Explore an interactive collection of line-based films, patterns, and playful visual experiments.

title: Checkbox Toys
description: A tiny checkbox Olympics where simple clicks turn into a surprisingly satisfying little browser challenge.

title: Line Rider
description: Draw a track and watch a tiny sledder glide, crash, loop, and somehow make your doodles feel dramatic.

title: Cameron's World
description: A living collage of rescued GeoCities-era internet fragments, packed with strange retro web energy.

title: Mikutap
description: Tap keys or click around to trigger bright animations and cheerful vocal sounds in a fast audiovisual toy.

title: The Revolving Internet
description: A famous web-art joke that makes the entire internet feel like it is spinning on a slow rotisserie.

title: Touch Pianist
description: Tap any keys to feel like you are performing classical piano without actually learning the piece.

title: Chris McKenzie Dotcom
description: A surreal interactive personal-site toy where moving your cursor becomes the whole strange point.

title: Bury Me With My Money
description: A tiny looping joke page with a falling figure, a quote, and no productive purpose whatsoever.

title: Much Better Than This
description: A simple animated loop that is somehow funny, soothing, and completely unnecessary at the same time.

title: Is My Computer On Fire?
description: A one-question website that answers a very specific worry with the confidence only a pointless page can have.

title: ClickClickClick
description: A nosy browser toy that comments on every click, pause, and tiny action as if the page is watching you.

title: Koalas to the Max
description: Move your cursor through shrinking circles until a hidden koala image slowly appears from the dots.

title: JacksonPollock.org
description: Splash colorful paint across the screen with only your cursor and make instant messy browser art.

title: Endless Horse
description: Scroll down an absurdly long ASCII horse whose legs keep going much farther than they should.

title: Sometimes Red Sometimes Blue
description: A minimal page that flips between red and blue, turning a tiny color change into the entire joke.

title: Typatone
description: Type words and hear them turn into a gentle melody with matching visual notes, a calm keyboard-powered sound toy.

title: Weird or Confusing
description: Press one button and get sent to a bizarre shopping-page oddity, perfect for a quick absurd internet detour.

title: Zoomquilt 2
description: An endless zooming illustrated world that feels hypnotic, strange, and oddly satisfying in the browser.

title: Google Gravity
description: A classic browser experiment where a familiar search page collapses into playful physics chaos.

title: Hooooooooo
description: A tiny one-button web toy that turns a simple click into a long, silly internet shout.

title: Sanger
description: A dog licks the inside of your screen. That is the whole joke, and it is still oddly watchable.

title: Checkboxland
description: A set of demos that turns ordinary HTML checkboxes into animations, drawings, and surprisingly playful browser art.

title: Sliding Toys
description: Slide tiny panels around the screen and enjoy a minimal puzzle-like toy with no setup or serious goal.

title: END CITY
description: A surreal browser place to wander through abstract rooms, odd visuals, and dreamlike interactive fragments.

title: Trash Loop
description: A hypnotic looping trash-can animation that turns a tiny visual joke into a strangely watchable page.

title: Nulling the Void
description: A one-purpose page for staring into a digital void, useful when the best boredom break is almost nothing.

title: Yes No If
description: A strange yes-or-no decision toy that gives quick absurd answers when you want the internet to choose for you.

title: One Square Minesweeper
description: A tiny one-square twist on Minesweeper that turns a familiar puzzle into a quick absurd browser game.

title: Crazy Card Trick
description: A classic quick card illusion that feels like a tiny magic trick hiding inside an old-school web page.

title: Doughnut Kitten
description: A delightfully silly doughnut-and-kitten page for a short cute internet detour with no setup at all.

title: Bouncing DVD Logo
description: Watch the nostalgic DVD logo bounce around the browser and wait for the corner hit you probably came for.

title: Semi-Conductor
description: A Google experiment where your webcam gestures conduct an AI-powered orchestra in the browser.
